title: Removal of gDNA from total RNA
author: Igem Dusseldorf
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8bfhsjn

steps.title: pipet components into a RNase-free 1
steps.instruction: pipet components into a RNase-free 1.5 ml tube (following order: H2O, buffer, total RNA, DNaseI)

steps.title: add 1 µl 50 mM EDTA...
steps.instruction: add 1 µl 50 mM EDTA and incubate at 65°C 00:10:00

steps.title: add 1/10 Volume NaOAc and 3 Volumes 100% EtOH
steps.instruction: add 1/10 Volume NaOAc and 3 Volumes 100% EtOH

steps.title: freeze at -20°C over night or...
steps.instruction: freeze at -20°C over night or at -80°C for at least 1h

steps.title: Discard supernatant RNA pellet may be...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant RNA pellet may be invisible, make sure to align tube lid so you remember where the pellet is located

steps.title: discard supernatant, remove as much ethanol as possible
steps.instruction: discard supernatant, remove as much ethanol as possible

steps.title: dry tubes at RT under the...
steps.instruction: dry tubes at RT under the hood (~5min), do not overdry!

steps.title: resuspend pellet in 20 µl RNase-free water
steps.instruction: resuspend pellet in 20 µl RNase-free water

steps.title: perform PCR on your DNA-free RNA
steps.instruction: perform PCR on your DNA-free RNA. Include a positive control using gDNA as a template and primer that bind to it. There should be no visible bands except for positive control!

steps.title: Calculate the volume required for 1...
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume required for 1 µg RNA %s %s ComponentamountDNaseI buffer1 µltotal RNA1 µgDNaseI1 µlH2Oto 10 µl
